source_file: [Day 19] Adventure Kit; 30 Days Lost in Space (English_ASR).srt
full_text: Rise and shine Explorer today the day we've been working towards we're finally ready to ascend from the ocean depths and glide our spaceship back up to the watery surface I hope you're as thrilled as I am the past few weeks have been a test of your courage determination and your ability to think on your feet and I must say you've handled every challenge with intelligence and tenacity you're proving to be a true Explorer in every sense but remember there is still much work to be done today we will put everything we've learned to the ultimate test all of your wiring coating and problem solving skills are going to play a crucial part in our Endeavor today so take a deep breath clear your mind and prepare yourself today you're more than an Explorer and an engineer you're a brave Starship Commander navigating tenaciously through the unknown strap in Explorer it's going to be a thrilling ride are you ready it appears we're all set to make our careful Ascent back up to the water surface it's critical we do this gradually to prevent our shuttle from popping like a balloon under the change and pressure do you recall our trusty sidekick the passive buzzer I'd suggest we reintroduce him into our circuit as an additional safety measure he'll be our alarm Bell warning us if we're ascending too quickly don't fret I've got you covered with all the guidance you'll need conveniently located in your mission resources and guess what they're ready for playback right now beginning playback hello everybody and welcome back to day 19 of the 30 days loss in space adventure kit code explanations now a lot of this code that we put in is from day 18 I'm not going to be going over that code from day 18 so what we can do we can simplify a lot of this by removing a lot tough comments and going down and explaining what's already known obviously we have these functions at the bottom nothing's changed there we have a lot of new functionality inside of our Loop but besides that there's actually not a whole lot of new things this is all the same from setup from yesterday I could actually just go ploop close that up not necessary we also have the constant bite done but we've also got nope and then we also have a new one for today called hold and as you can guess it works the exact same way as the other two so I'm just going to close that down and that's going to be another one that we can show off later on once we show off our code we have the keys make sure you have these in here if you don't it will not work same as up above the only new thing that we have up above for this entire top part is we have the constant bite buzzer pin is going to be equal to 10 today we're going to be introducing a buzzer so that we can have additional alert sounds whenever we hit certain depths so that we know not to rise too quickly back up to the surface now what we can do is we can actually instead of using those variables like we had yesterday we can actually use percentages so we know that we're going to have to stop at about 50% of the way and 75% of the way so we want to alert the user when we have risen to 50 and 75% of the way up so instead of before we had -20 and -40 we're going to be using this instead this is the same we also have a constant unsigned int Loop delay equals 200 which is 200 milliseconds that we're going to add as a small little buffer for our interrupts between the loop executions inside of the loop we have the following static in previous depth is equal to initial depth and this is just going to give us our depth from our previous Loop now if there was a change obviously this is similar same thing as yesterday if there was a change in the depth level meaning you turn that rotary encoder at some amount and resist us at least a little bit probably just one if there was any change in that we're going to go through this Loop grabbing the current depth just like yesterday and then we're going to compute our percentage of the way up now yesterday we were saying what the depth level was but instead we're going to give us a percentage from zero to 100 in order to calculate that we can take the current depth time 100 divided by the initial depth and do 100 minus that entire total that will give us the percentage of the way to the surface that we've gone so far now Rising too quickly could stress the whole of our Lander because of this we will play an alert if the Lander is instructed to rise faster than one meter every time through our Loop we need to be sure that we're not going too quickly so let's just say you went through the loop and you spun it really quick and so you went from one to four all that one second hey we're going to have a buzzer pin go off that tells us that we need to chill out for just a minute you're going a little too fast this is the same logic as before which simply just says if the current depth is less than the initial depth don't try going down because Mr wise guy we then have the the function to show that number whatever the depth is on our s segment display and then we finally have the same thing as before if we've hit the very first 50% Mark or the 75% Mark of our way up to the surface we want to display our depth is a little blink on the display and finally we're going to display done assuming that it's you make it all the way up to the surface the only thing that we're doing here we're going to play a little Tada tune on our buzzer that's that there and that's the only new feature that's being added that we haven't already had from yesterday and with that that is now the end of day 19 I wish you the best of luck today on the way up to the ocean surface light speed welcome to the day 19 wiring example video well the big day has finally arrived D all of our designing testing and practicing has been leading up to this crucial step in getting home raising our craft to the ocean surface based on the day 18 project we now have a design that adds an audio warning buzzer to the encoder and display systems that we've previously worked out looking at our breadboard we can see that the only Hardware change from day 18 is this buzzer right here and we can see that the buzzer is connected to the hero at output pin 10 so pin 10 should be the pin in our sketch that drives the buzzer function and so you can check that and make sure that that's the case and you'll also see that our buzzer has to have a ground connection and we're just using the same Common Ground bus connection right here on the bottom side of our breadboard that's already being used by the encoder and the display the really substantial changes today mostly involve the software sketch last time when we were simulating and practicing we could afford to make mistakes and then just go back and try again until we got it right but this time we'll need to be extra careful not to irreversibly damage our crafts Drive systems and Hull and hopefully get it right on the first try because of this the importance of this particular day project our sketch has warning logic built in that is intended to alert us both audibly and Visually if we are ascending too fast and it will also tell us when there are planned pause points in the ascent that we've reached now I'll go ahead and run my simulation of the real thing so that as you're watching you'll know just what to expect when it's go time for your run to the surface so let's go ahead load up the sketch and see how it goes so I'm going over to the IDE now loading up the uh the sketch in just a moment we should be running and there we are okay so our sketch is running and our display is indicating that we are currently at a depth of 60 M and we've got to get up to the surface when our uh depth meter reaches zero so if everything's ready to go let's go ahead put our hands on the controls and see what happens so as I start to rotate the encoder you can see that I'm decreasing the the depth I'm rising up in other words and if I go a little too fast I get my my fingers slapped a little bit because going up too fast is putting too much stress on my drive okay that was too fast still going up okay I've reached a hold Point all right so I I've reached a hold point I was still going a little fast there because you can see I overshot the hold point if I wanted to I could go back down a little bit but let's keep going up and see what happens as I okay that was too fast and anytime now there I've reached another hold point at 15 M okay after pausing at the hold Point let's keep rising up up up and we should be close enough to the surface now oh that was too fast we're almost there 5 4 3 2 1 and we did it we've reached the surface so of course that was just a simulation of what you're going to do for real but it's just as simple as that so I wish you good luck and we will see you top side after you've made your way out of the depths and then you'll be ready to prepare for the next part of the journey home see you then okay Explorer the time has come all our preparations have led us to this point our Ascent out of the murky deep and back to the surface of this Uncharted ocean we will slowly ascend from our current depth at altitude zero to the ocean surface of altitude 100 the key word is slowly we must proceed cautiously to prevent an explosion which will surely put a hasty end to our journey on my mark Explorer start turning the rotary encoder and let's begin our Ascent to the surface slow and steady engage all system status clear keeping tabs on gyroscopic thrusters pressure values well within acceptable limits steady on Explorer 50% slow your rooll Explorer the ship's pressure is reaching its limits we need to stop for a moment occupy your mind with thoughts of the world Beyond this frighteningly vast ocean a world filled with more breathable air and fewer leviathans pressure is within optimal levels please continue to turn our encoder and continue the ascent 75% you're three4 to the top Explorer but don't lose your patience halt your dial turning for another few moments we're almost there sensors indicate light is coming through much more clearly now okay pressure dropping Crank That encoder Cadet you've done it Explorer you've reached the ocean surface I'm an awe Explorer what an extraordinary achievement today we've navigated our way from the murky depth to the Magnificent spectacle of a sky teeming with distant Stars the view is simply mesmerizing isn't it our Collective efforts have truly paid off and look at that the communications with the Mother Ship have stabilized I can almost feel the stream of signals pulsating through the system each beep echoing the missed inquiries from headquarters they must be on tenter hooks awaiting an update from you eager to hear that you're safe and sound it's time to let them in on our Victory to share the wave of hope that's been washing over us take a moment relish this feeling and then let's send a reassuring Beacon back home oh I could just leap with joy Explorer what a day what a Triumph light speed to you and remember every Star in the cosmos is a destination waiting to be explored
